channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Niger says soldiers, civilians killed in armed attack on Niamey airport
item.description: Ministry of Defence says 11 soldiers, 2 civilians and 22 attackers killed in Diori Hamani International Airport assault.

item.title: US progressive Lewis George on track to become DC mayor after Trump threats
item.description: The Democratic Socialist has vowed aggressive response to Trump, who has said he could 'take back' DC if she wins.

item.title: Continuity or change? What to know about Colombia’s run-off election
item.description: Far-right Abelardo de la Espriella and leftist Ivan Cepeda compete for the presidency on starkly different platforms.

item.title: How does Trump’s MOU with Iran compare with Obama’s nuclear pact?
item.description: Trump promised a better deal than the 2015 JCPOA nuclear agreement with Iran - but is it?

item.title: World Cup: Ivory Coast’s Elye Wahi denied Canada entry for Germany match
item.description: Wahi was unauthorised for travel after he was reportedly arrested last month in a French spot-fixing investigation.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Day seven
item.description: Al Jazeera’s @RahulP_Journo has your #FIFAworldcup26 day 7 recap.

item.title: Trump-Netanyahu tensions: Have Israeli and US leaders clashed before?
item.description: Media reports talk about rifts between Israeli and American leaders, but it has not changed US policy towards Israel.

item.title: These are the Palestinians that Israel has killed during Gaza’s ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Israel has killed more than 1,000 Palestinians since agreeing to a US-brokered ‘ceasefire’ in its genocidal war.

item.title: Patients die in Gaza waiting for medical evacuations Israel keeps blocking
item.description: Despite having medical referrals to leave Gaza, Palestinians are not allowed to leave.

item.title: What went wrong for Cristiano Ronaldo in his first World Cup 2026 match?
item.description: Messi, Mbappe, Haaland and Kane began the World Cup in style, but what happened to Ronaldo and Portugal?

item.title: UK court convicts two men for spying for Hong Kong, China
item.description: The men, dual Chinese-British nationals, are believed to be the first people convicted of spying for China ‌in Britain.

item.title: Rial rebounds and stocks soar, but Iranians still grapple with high prices
item.description: After an initial US-Iran agreement is reached, Iranians still await relief at the grocery checkout.

item.title: In Britain, resisting a genocide is now treated as terrorism
item.description: The Filton 4 case reveals a democracy more frightened of protesters than of the destruction they protest against.

item.title: Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon kill three despite US-Iran deal
item.description: Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon continue in spite of a US-Iran deal that would mean ceasing military operations.

item.title: South Africa vs Czechia LIVE: World Cup 2026
item.description: Follow the build-up, analysis and live text commentary of the game as South Africa and Czechia face off in Group A.

item.title: Pentagon chief announces review of US forces in Europe, slams NATO allies
item.description: Pete Hegseth says the review would last for up to six months and include consultations with Congress.

item.title: Iran says it will charge a ‘payment for services’ in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran says it will charge a ‘payment for services’ in the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: World Cup 2026: Key takeaways from the opening group stage matches
item.description: From viral fan moments to on-field controversies, as well as the biggest stars, best performances, upsets and more.

item.title: What the Trump-Iran agreement says about Lebanon, Hormuz and uranium
item.description: The memorandum of understanding covers 14 points but leaves many crucial questions unanswered.

item.title: Greece’s ‘war on Roma’ is Europe’s new blueprint for discrimination
item.description: Mass raids on Roma communities show how Europe is recasting racial discrimination as crime prevention and public order.

item.title: Real Madrid sign Ibrahima Konate to multi-year deal from Liverpool
item.description: Acquisition of the French defender, who is currently at the World Cup, is a key signing by new Real coach Jose Mourinho.

item.title: Apple CEO warns price rises ‘unavoidable’ amid AI boom
item.description: The increase of AI data centres has made supply for key electronic device components dwindle, driving up prices.

item.title: World reacts to US-Iran deal to extend ceasefire, reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Several countries welcome deal, as IAEA chief says 'now the technical work starts' on Iran's nuclear programme.

item.title: Hegseth slams NATO allies during roundtable meeting
item.description: Hegseth slams NATO allies during roundtable meeting

item.title: Taiwan hopes US arms sale package can be approved soon, president says
item.description: Taiwan’s President William Lai Ching-te reiterates his desire for talks based on 'parity and respect' with China.
